data.title: Boralex CFO Bruno Guilmette to depart after 7 years; will stay until Sep 12, 2025 for transition.
data.description: In Montreal on August 8, 2025, Boralex Inc. declared that Bruno Guilmette, Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer, is leaving the company after almost seven years to pursue a new venture. Guilmette, who has been with Boralex since [year], will continue as CFO until September 12, 2025, to facilitate a seamless handover. Boralex Inc. is listed on the Toronto Stock Exchange under the ticker symbol BLX.

data.title: Boralex appoints André Courville as Chair of the Board, replacing Alain Rhéaume after 15 years, 8 as Chair.
data.description: On August 8, 2025, Boralex Inc. revealed the selection of Mr. André Courville as the new Chair of the Board of Directors. This appointment came after the retirement announcement of Mr. Alain Rhéaume in June. Rhéaume had dedicated 15 years to the company, including eight years as the Chair. Courville is set to take over on September 30. Boralex Inc. is listed on the Toronto Stock Exchange under the ticker symbol BLX.

data.title: Boralex reports Q2 operating income similar to 2024, remains active in development; TSX: BLX.
data.description: In Montreal, on August 8, 2025, Boralex Inc. (TSX: BLX) announced its second-quarter results for 2025. The Corporation expressed contentment in reporting its performance during this period.

data.title: Heron Therapeutics reports Q2 2025 net revenue of $37.2M, YTD revenue of $76.1M, and expects 2025 net revenue of $153M-$163M. YTD 2025 Adjusted EBITDA is $7.9M.
data.description: In the second quarter of 2025, there was a net revenue of $37.2 million generated, with a year-to-date revenue totaling $76.1 million. The net revenue guidance for 2025 remains at $153 million to $163 million. Furthermore, a record year-to-date 2025 adjusted EBITDA of $7.9 million was reported, leading to an increase in the full-year 2025 adjusted EBITDA guidance from $4.0 million.

data.title: Cryptocurrency market surges on regulatory boost, ETF inflows. Bitcoin at $116,832.44, XRP at $3.32, Dogecoin at $0.2231.
data.description: The cryptocurrency markets saw a positive uptick on Friday following favorable regulatory developments, leading to increased ETF inflows and growing institutional adoption sentiment. Bitcoin (BTC) was priced at $116,832.44, Ethereum (ETH) at $3,908.71, Solana (SOL) at $177.28, XRP at $3.32, Dogecoin (DOGE) at $0.2231, and Shiba Inu (SHIB) at $0.00001294. Notable statistics include a 2.4% decrease in large transactions for Bitcoin and a 15.7% drop for Ethereum in a single day. Daily active addresses for Bitcoin dipped by 1.7%, while Ethereum saw a 1.3% increase. 103,582 traders were liquidated in the past 24 hours for a total of $300.68 million, as per Coinglass data. SoSoValue data indicates net inflows of $280.7 million from spot Bitcoin ETFs on Wednesday, with spot Ethereum ETFs seeing net inflows of $222.3 million.

data.title: Gold Reserve updates responses to objections regarding CITGO sale process, Dalinar Energy Corp selected.
data.description: In Bermuda, Gold Reserve Ltd. (TSX.V: GRZ) (BSX: GRZ.BH) (OTCQX: GDRZF) has issued an announcement regarding the objections to the Special Master’s Final Recommendation. According to the U.S. District Court for the District of Delaware, multiple parties have submitted memoranda and statements in response to the recommendation that Dalinar Energy Corporation, a U.S. acquisition subsidiary of Gold Reserve, be chosen by the Court for the acquisition of shares.

data.title: London Blockchain Conference on Oct 22-23, 2025, follows UK Government Summit, focusing on blockchain regulation and public-private strategy.
data.description: A conference in London on October 22nd-23rd, 2025 is set to expand on the UK Government Summit held at the House of Lords on July 1st. The event will focus on addressing issues related to blockchain regulation, scaling, and developing public-private strategies. This conference follows the successful third Government Summit and is expected to draw significant participation and attention.

data.title: Block Inc.'s Bitcoin holdings soar to nearly $1 billion, with an 81% year-over-year increase, recording a $212.17 million remeasurement gain under new accounting rules.
data.description: Block Inc., listed as XYZ on NYSE, has seen a significant surge in the value of its Bitcoin investment, reaching close to a billion dollars. The company disclosed in their second quarter 2025 financial report that their Bitcoin treasury has grown by over 81% year-over-year. The investment, held for long-term reasons, rose from $514.584 million in June 2024 to $931.656 million by June 2025. This increase led to a remeasurement gain of $212.17 million in the second quarter of 2025, a notable shift from the $70.12 million remeasurement loss in the corresponding period the previous year. This change is attributed to new accounting rules, categorizing Bitcoin as an "indefinite-lived intangible asset." This information originates from Benzinga.com.

data.title: Odd Burger posted record revenue of $1,044,646 in Q3 2025, with positive EBITDA of $40,407, marking significant growth.
data.description: Odd Burger Corporation, a vegan fast-food chain, reported its financial results for the third quarter ended June 30, 2025, showing impressive growth. The company posted its highest quarterly revenue ever at $1,044,646, up 42.8% from the previous quarter and 18.8% from the same period last year. They achieved positive EBITDA of $40,407, a significant improvement from a negative EBITDA of $(242,019) in the previous quarter. The gross margin increased to $443,575 (42.5%) from 34.0% in the previous quarter, while the net loss decreased to $(147,905), a 60% improvement. Salaries & wages were reduced by 57% compared to the same quarter last year, and SG&A expenses decreased by $130,569 from the previous quarter. CEO James McInnes stated that achieving positive EBITDA was a crucial milestone, indicating the company's ability to scale efficiently. The results reflect growing demand for their plant-based offerings and efficient operational execution.
